It's the residence of the Danish royal family.
It's a famous example of modern architecture.
It's a character from Grimm's Fairy Tales.
It's a world-famous restaurant that offers culinary masterpieces.
It's an astronomy tower from the 17th century.
It's one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros.
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro.
The weather in Copenhagen in December is below -15 degrees Celsius.
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.
The “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” in Danish is “farvel”, “goodbye” in Danish is “tak”.
